label: SES-115 / CNV-017 close-out — PI-102: reconcile executive_summary nullability across the SQLAlchemy models and the live database (commit 73e4f08). Authors DEC-330 (full-stack reconcile, no new migration). Resolves PI-102. Attaches to WS-012.

session.session_title: PI-102 — reconcile executive_summary nullability between the SQLAlchemy models and the live database (full-stack reconcile)
session.session_description:
Single Claude Code execution at Doug's terminal (CNV-017). Parent workstream WS-012 (Parallel agent orchestrator and executive summary). Medium: chat via Claude Code. Seed prompt: "review pi-102 and plan how to execute the pi", then "start executing", then "organize the closeout payload and execute".

PI-102 (filed by SES-111 via DEP-104/COP-111) flagged that models.py declared decisions.executive_summary nullable while the live CRMBUILDER database enforces NOT NULL with a 200-800 char CHECK (tightened by migration 0023 / PI-075). The SQLAlchemy source no longer described the actual schema.

Work, in order:
1. Investigation: confirmed the live DB (CRMBUILDER.db, Alembic head) has NOT NULL + 200-800 CHECK with no IS NULL arm on decisions, planning_items, and sessions; the models still carried nullable=True with a permissive 'IS NULL OR (length...)' CHECK. Established that engagement DBs are built by running the Alembic chain (already correct) while test DBs use create_all from the models (wrongly nullable). Resolved PI-102's open question: NO new migration — 0023 already formalized it; this is the source-of-record catch-up.
2. Full-stack reconcile (Doug chose full-stack over model-only): models.py three columns -> Mapped[str], nullable=False, CHECK drops the IS NULL arm; new validate_required_length helper; decisions / planning_items / sessions create paths require executive_summary, update/patch require a valid value when present; API create bodies + SessionReplaceIn require it (partial update/patch stay optional); the three MCP create tools take a required executive_summary; the three UI dialog fields become required=True. conversations.conversation_executive_summary is left nullable — migration 0023 deliberately excluded it (not yet backfilled).
3. Test reconciliation: established a baseline at b13707c (105 pre-existing access+api failures and 17 ui+mcp failures, unrelated to PI-102 — old session shapes, commit/conversation/deposit machinery, a dangling import). Fixed the 68 access+api and 29 ui+mcp delta tests by threading a valid summary through their create/POST/dialog sites; verified the suites return exactly to baseline with zero new failures. Added test_executive_summary_required.py (access + api) locking in the NOT-NULL schema shape, access-layer rejection, the 422/400 API contract, and that conversations stays nullable.
4. Authored DEC-330. Lint clean on all changed files.

The code deliverable is commit 73e4f08 on branch pi-102-exec-summary-nullability (an isolated worktree based on the clean b13707c, not main's 'abandoned commit'), pending Doug's push and merge. This governance close-out was applied from the main repo because the worktree's db-export baseline (b13707c) is stale relative to the live DB; the db-export diff here is exactly the PI-102 records.

In flight at end: commit 73e4f08 awaits Doug's push/merge.
session.session_executive_summary: Reconciled the executive_summary column's nullability between the SQLAlchemy source and the live governance database, which has enforced NOT NULL with a 200-800 character CHECK since migration 0023 (PI-075). The models, access-layer create paths, API create schemas, MCP create tools, and the UI dialog fields were all brought into agreement so the source accurately describes the schema and omitting the summary now yields a clean 422/400 instead of a database 500. conversations.conversation_executive_summary was intentionally left nullable (migration 0023 excluded it). No new migration was needed. Resolves PI-102.

Resolved the PI's open question with no new migration. Updated 97 affected tests to zero regressions against baseline and added dedicated contract tests. Authored DEC-330. Resolves PI-102. conversations left nullable pending its own backfill.

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-102 — reconcile executive_summary nullability across source layers
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-102 — reconcile executive_summary nullability across source layers

The live CRMBUILDER database has enforced NOT NULL + a 200-800 char CHECK
on executive_summary (decisions, planning_items) and
session_executive_summary (sessions) since migration 0023 (PI-075), but
the SQLAlchemy models still declared the columns nullable with a
permissive "IS NULL OR (length...)" CHECK. The model — and the access,
schema, MCP, and UI layers built on it — disagreed with the database it
describes. This reconciles all of them to the live schema. No new
migration: 0023 already formalized the constraint; this is the
source-of-record catch-up.

- models.py: three columns -> Mapped[str], nullable=False; CHECK drops
  the IS NULL arm. conversations.conversation_executive_summary is left
  nullable (migration 0023 deliberately excluded it; not yet backfilled).
- _helpers.py: add validate_required_length (rejects None/empty/short).
- decisions / planning_items / sessions repos: create paths now require
  executive_summary; update/patch require a valid value when the key is
  present (a NOT NULL column cannot be cleared). Turns an omitted summary
  into a clean 400/422 instead of a DB IntegrityError (500).
- api/schemas.py: create bodies + SessionReplaceIn (PUT) require the
  field; partial update/patch bodies stay optional.
- mcp_server/tools.py: create_decision / create_planning_item /
  create_session tools take a required executive_summary argument.
- UI schemas (decision / planning_item / session): Executive Summary
  field is required=True (was required=False + omit_when_empty_in_create),
  making the form honest about the NOT NULL constraint.
- tests: thread a valid summary through the affected create/POST/dialog
  sites; add test_executive_summary_required.py (access + api) locking in
  the NOT-NULL schema shape, access-layer rejection, the 422/400 API
  contract, and that conversations stays nullable.

Verified: full access+api and ui+mcp suites show zero new failures vs the
b13707c baseline; the 14 new contract tests pass.

Governance close-out (resolving PI-102 in the DB via close-out payload +
apply_close_out.py) is deferred: apply_close_out is currently broken on
this base for the PI-073 session shape.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

decisions.title: Reconcile executive_summary nullability to the live schema across all source layers — no new migration (PI-102)
decisions.context: PI-102 found models.py declaring executive_summary nullable on decisions (and the same drift on planning_items and sessions) while migration 0023 (PI-075) had already tightened the live columns to NOT NULL with a 200-800 character CHECK and no IS NULL arm. The SQLAlchemy source — and the access, API-schema, MCP-tool, and UI layers built on it — disagreed with the database they describe. Engagement DBs run the Alembic chain (already correct); test DBs use create_all from the models, so they were wrongly nullable.
decisions.decision: Reconcile every source layer to the live schema rather than add a new migration (0023 already formalized the constraint). models.py: the three columns become Mapped[str], nullable=False, with the CHECK's IS NULL arm removed. Add a validate_required_length helper; the decisions, planning_items, and sessions create paths require executive_summary, and update/patch require a valid value when the key is present (a NOT NULL column cannot be cleared). API create bodies and SessionReplaceIn require the field; partial update/patch bodies stay optional. The three MCP create tools take a required executive_summary argument. The three UI dialog fields become required=True. conversations.conversation_executive_summary is deliberately left nullable because migration 0023 excluded it pending its own backfill.
decisions.rationale: The source of record must match the database it describes, or future authors are misled. Making the create paths required converts a silent database IntegrityError (HTTP 500) into a clean validation error (422 for an omitted field, 400 for an out-of-range value). A full-stack reconcile keeps all five layers consistent rather than leaving the access/schema/MCP/UI layers permissive while only the model is correct.
decisions.alternatives_considered: (a) Add a new Alembic migration to formalize the constraint — rejected: 0023 already did it; a second migration would be a redundant no-op on the live DB. (b) Only correct the model's nullable flag — rejected: it still breaks ~40 create-path tests and leaves the access/schema/MCP/UI layers permissive and the 500-vs-422 gap open. (c) Defer entirely — rejected: the drift is a standing correctness/maintainability defect in the source of record.
decisions.consequences: Callers of the decision/planning_item/session create paths must now supply a 200-800 character executive_summary; ~68 access+api and ~29 ui+mcp tests were updated and a dedicated contract test file was added. Fresh test DBs (create_all) now match the live engagement DBs. conversations.conversation_executive_summary remains nullable until a future PI backfills it and tightens it. Delivered in commit 73e4f08 on branch pi-102-exec-summary-nullability, pending push and merge.
decisions.executive_summary: PI-102 found the SQLAlchemy models declaring executive_summary nullable while migration 0023 (PI-075) had already tightened the live columns to NOT NULL with a 200-800 char CHECK. Rather than add a redundant migration, this decision reconciles every source layer to the live schema: model nullable=False plus a no-NULL-arm CHECK, a new validate_required_length helper backing required create paths in the access layer, required create-body fields in the API and MCP tool schemas, and required=True on the three UI dialog fields. It converts a silent database 500 on an omitted summary into a clean 422/400. conversations.conversation_executive_summary stays nullable because migration 0023 deliberately excluded it pending its own backfill.
